raw.population: N: 52 (45% from African/Indian patients) Sex: m=73%, f=55% Age: 7.6 ±4.2 (1.2-16) WT: 24.2 ±12.5
raw.objective: “to use NONMEM to derive a model describing valproate clearance in terms of individual patient characteristics. Such an individualized clearance could then be used to predict the maintenance dose required to achieve a target mean steady-state serum concentration”
raw.covariates: Tested: SEX, RACE, AGE, Comedication (CBZ, PHT, PHB, Included: WT and comedication CBZ
raw.observations: PHB and PHT were expected to increase VPA CL, but was not proven, likely due to sample N taking comedication. Ka was fixed; Vd was excluded as variance was very small and did not alter parameter values. With increasing age the weight-adjusted VPA CL decreased. No PRED vs DV plots are made to ensure accuracy of model.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: One-compartment model, first-order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL=(exp((theta1*WT)-theta2)*(theta4**(1-M)))*(1+eta1) V = theta5*WT KA=theta3 Cobs=Cipred +eps1 M: Scaling factor for comedication (1:VPA/VPA+PHB/PHT, 1.61:VPA+CMZ) Value of V based on VPA PK model by Dutta et al.
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 0.022 theta2= 1.38 theta3=1.9 theta4=1.61 theta6=1.81 omega1=0.0265 sigma1 = 0.0605

extraction_target.title: A model for estimating individualized valproate clearance values in children.
extraction_target.population: N: 52 (45% from African/Indian patients) Sex: m=73%, f=55% Age: 7.6 ±4.2 (1.2-16) WT: 24.2 ±12.5

structural_model.description: One-compartment model, first-order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL=(exp((theta1*WT)-theta2)*(theta4**(1-M)))*(1+eta1) V = theta5*WT KA=theta3 Cobs=Cipred +eps1 M: Scaling factor for comedication (1:VPA/VPA+PHB/PHT, 1.61:VPA+CMZ) Value of V based on VPA PK model by Dutta et al.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.population: N: 52 (45% from African/Indian patients) Sex: m=73%, f=55% Age: 7.6 ±4.2 (1.2-16) WT: 24.2 ±12.5
study.objective: “to use NONMEM to derive a model describing valproate clearance in terms of individual patient characteristics. Such an individualized clearance could then be used to predict the maintenance dose required to achieve a target mean steady-state serum concentration”

covariates.raw: Tested: SEX, RACE, AGE, Comedication (CBZ, PHT, PHB, Included: WT and comedication CBZ

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
